cards.title: Tame Your AI Bills: The Fable 5 Pricing Nightmare is Your Goldmine
cards.thesis: People are absolutely loving cutting-edge AI models like Claude's Fable 5, but they're hitting a wall with confusing pricing, vague limits, and unexpected costs. There's a clear demand for tools that give users more control and transparency over their AI usage and spending, moving beyond simple chat interfaces to more integrated 'work apps.'

evidence.text: People are building open-source, local-first alternatives to apps like Claude Desktop, wanting something 'less like a chat app and more like a full-fledged work app.' (Rowboat)

When you build something people REALLY want, you can pull off ridiculous stunts like Claude's pricing: vague limits, confusing usage stats, and a $200/month subscription that doesn't even include Fable 5. People are frustrated but hooked.
People are asking if anyone is 'keeping [Fable 5] once it's usage-billed?' after the promotional access ends, highlighting concern over costs.

There's a general frustration with large platforms and their opaque billing, like being 'shocked to find out that I was going to be charged ~£250' for cancelling an Adobe CC subscription early due to a yearly contract.
The high engagement (385) on 'Davit, a Apple Containers UI' which was 'mostly vibe-coded' shows a strong interest in well-designed, user-friendly interfaces even for complex developer tools.

cards.opportunity: Everyone's hooked on Claude's Fable 5, but they're absolutely furious about its 'ridiculous' pricing, vague limits, and the shift to usage-based billing. While Rowboat is building a local alternative, there's a massive opening for a simple, beautiful dashboard that connects to your Claude account (via its API – a way for different software to talk to each other) and visually tracks your Fable 5 usage and spending in real-time. Ship a basic version this weekend that just shows daily usage and estimated cost, and you'll own the market of users desperate to avoid bill shock and gain control.
